{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"api_error_status":null,"duration_ms":497755,"duration_api_ms":497752,"ttft_ms":6785,"num_turns":52,"result":"Review complete. Verdict: **PASS_WITH_FINDINGS** (no P0/P1/P2; one P3 advisory).

CP00-894 is a clean descriptor-only RP29 Commercial Readiness review-scope bridge. By direct source inspection I confirmed:
- Binding, deliverable distribution, and micro-title distribution match the baseline exactly (recount = 150).
- No-write/no-real-data boundaries are hard-enforced on every row, and the validators/tests/contracts back this.
- Claude is correctly read-only and non-final-approver; Hermes is descriptor-only with no product runtime.
- The pack honestly reports `production_ready=false` pending review — no overclaim — and the 0-byte review placeholder is correctly not treated as a valid receipt.
- Changed-file scope is clean; unrelated dirty/untracked files (including the unrelated LDIP BLOCK result) were correctly excluded and did not influence the verdict.

The single P3 advisory documents that the full 10-command validation ladder and human approval are downstream gates not yet recorded, so this read-only PASS is evidence-scoped — closeout/commit remains correctly blocked until those pass. `blocks_pack_closeout=false`, `blocks_goal_closeout=false`. Direct read-only inspection supports the pack's descriptor-verified claim and confirms the production_ready claim is NOT overstated: manifest, command-evidence, construction-inspection, adjudication, and claude-review-result all consistently report production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false / pending_review. VERIFIED BY SOURCE INSPECTION: (1) COMMERCIAL_CP894_PACK_BINDING (registry.js:422) matches the baseline exactly — risk C, 150 units, range, upstream CP893, next CP895, flag commercial_readiness_review_scope_bridge_descriptor_verified; (2) COMMERCIAL_CP894_REQUIREMENTS deliverable_counts {hermes_evidence:7,claude_review:26,security_audit:19,test:8,ui:8,implementation:82} and micro_title_row_counts match the baseline distributions exactly (independent recount = 150); (3) the descriptor sets authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, and every Hermes-evidence/review-packet row hard-sets descriptor_only=true with runtime_execution, permission_decision_written, audit_event_written, runtime_receipt_emitted, real_client_data_included, credential_or_secret_included all false; (4) validators.js comprehensively enforces coverage drift, per-row no-write/no-runtime, descriptor type, capabilities, safety gates, and contract projection, and the validator script exits non-zero on any error; (5) model.test.js covers binding/coverage/descriptor-bridge/contract-projection/compatibility; (6) contracts (validation.valid=true, no_write_attestation, alias latest_pack_id=CP00-894) are consistent with the registry; (7) Hermes patterns are review packet/receipt descriptors only (emits_runtime_receipt=false, hermes_command_executes_now=false) — no product-runtime execution; (8) Claude is correctly a read-only, non-final-approver (read_only=true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required=true). The empty (0-byte) review placeholder in claude-review-result.json is correctly marked pending and is NOT treated as a valid receipt (valid_review_receipts=[], exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run=false), satisfying CP178+ hardening. Changed-file scope is clean: the 7 dirty modified files all map to active_pack_implementation_refs, and unrelated dirty/untracked files (progress-control-room, workbook/*, LDIP review result, new-session-handoff) are correctly excluded — I did not let them influence this verdict. PROVENANCE NOTE: I inspected source and artifacts directly via Read/Grep/Glob but, being read-only, did not re-run any command; the passing exit codes in command-evidence.json are agent-reported. No P0/P1/P2 defects found. One P3 advisory: the full 10-command deterministic validation ladder and human final approval are downstream gates not yet recorded (command-evidence shows only initial targeted validations as passed, gate_outcome=pending_pack_review_and_full_validation), so this read-only PASS is evidence-scoped and pack closeout/commit remains correctly blocked_until_review_and_full_validation. blocks_goal_closeout=false: this CP keeps runtime/enterprise-trust explicitly closed and does not claim them, so the expected human-approval boundary is outside this receipt.","findings":[{"id":"CP894-ADV-01","severity":"P3","title":"Full deterministic validation ladder and human approval are downstream gates not yet recorded; PASS is evidence-scoped","file":"docs/closeout-packs/cp00-894/command-evidence.json","line":9,"details":"command-evidence.json records only initial targeted validations as passed (commercial_syntax_check_initial, commercial_model_service_tests_initial, rp29_commercial_validate_initial — all exit_code 0), with status=initial_validation_passed_pending_review, gate_outcome=pending_pack_review_and_full_validation, and hermes_gate.status=pending_full_ladder. The full 10-command ladder expected before closeout (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) is not yet recorded as passing. This is by design (the manifest commit_policy is blocked_until_review_and_full_validation and pack_level_claude_review precedes full validation), and the pack honestly reports production_ready=false, so there is NO overclaim. As a read-only reviewer I could not re-run these commands; the recorded exit codes are agent-reported and not independently re-executed.","recommendation":"Before promoting production_ready or committing closeout, ensure the full deterministic validation ladder passes and is recorded, and that human final approval is obtained.
